texto: If you read one book a week, starting at the age of 5, and live to be 80, you will have read a grand total of 3,900 books, a little over one-tenth of 1 percent of the books currently in print.
explicacao: Citação de Lewis Buzbee sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “If you read one book a week, starting at the age of 5, and live to be 80, you will have read a gr…” articula vida, books, reading em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como reading.

texto: I've had many more thousands of books in my possession than my shelves at home would indicate. At one time, I tried to keep them all, but that quest soon became impossible; I now only keep the ones I'm sure I'm going to reread, the ones I'm definitely going to read before I die, and the ones I can't bear to part with because of an aesthetic or emotional attachment.
explicacao: Citação de Lewis Buzbee sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I've had many more thousands of books in my possession than my shelves at home would indicate. At…” articula reflexao, books, going em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

At one time, I tried to keep them all, but that quest soon became impossible; I now only keep the ones I'm sure I'm going to reread, the ones I'm definitely going to read before I die, and the ones I can't bear to part with because of an aesthetic or emotional attachment.

texto: A bran' new book is a beautiful thing, all promise and fresh pages, the neatly squared spine, the brisk sense of a journey beginning. But a well-worn book also has its pleasures, the soft caress and give of the paper's edges, the comfort, like an old shawl, of an oft-read story.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lewis Buzbee: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “A bran' new book is a beautiful thing, all promise and fresh pages, the neatly squared spine, the…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, book-reading, books pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e books.
